description: Bloated class handling pipeline execution, metrics collection, and DLQ management.
reasoning: This class encapsulates multiple unrelated responsibilities: pipeline lifecycle management, complex metrics initialization and storage (L546-L780), Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) writer lifecycle (L387-L458), and utility logic like time duration parsing (L420). Changes to the metrics system or DLQ implementation would force changes to this core pipeline class.

description: God class managing queue logic, disk I/O, and resource monitoring.
reasoning: The Queue class handles high-level queue operations (read/write/ack), low-level page management (L185-L211), file locking/concurrency (L204, L823), serialization (L781), and physical disk space monitoring (L917). These represent separate concerns (logic, persistence, and resource management) that should be decoupled.

description: Data entity class mixed with serialization and formatting logic.
reasoning: While primarily a data container, the class also assumes responsibility for JSON parsing/serialization (L284-L314), binary serialization (L541), memory estimation (L577), and complex string interpolation (L397). Serialization formats and data structure management are distinct reasons for change.

description: Class mixing write operations with retention policy management and scheduling.
reasoning: The class is responsible for writing entries to the DLQ, but also contains logic for age-based and size-based retention policies (L458, L489), segment cleanup, file locking, and managing its own background flusher via an internal scheduler service. Retention management is a separate administrative concern from the writer's primary task.

description: Ruby Agent class acting as a God Object for the entire system.
reasoning: This class (and its associated top-level functions) manages pipeline lifecycles, webserver initialization (L481), metric collector configuration (L510), state convergence logic (L382), and geoip database metrics (L660). It ties together too many unrelated subsystems.

description: Compiler class mixed with runtime dataset implementations.
reasoning: The class contains the logic to compile datasets but also defines internal runtime structures like 'Complement' (L486) and 'ComputeAndClear' (L547). The logic for generating/compiling execution code should be separated from the operational behavior of the resulting dataset objects.
